system.description:
Ork culture is a seething morass of violence where the strong constantly assert their position over upstart, ambitious young orks. Only the largest, toughest, and most aggressive Orks rise to leadership amongst the greenskins. This makeshift 'nobility' results in a class of ork known as 'Nobz' in their guttural language. Nobz lead by example, fighting where the fighting is thickest and 'encouraging' any recalcitrant Orks or Grots with cuffs and blows to get them into the battle.

In many warbands, Nobz gain a favoured status for collecting loot or special wargear from the Meks, such as mega-armour. Such mega-armoured Nobz (known as Meganobz) often form an elite bodyguard for an Ork Warboss and accompany him into the heart of any fight with savage enthusiasm.

Gear: 5d10 Ork Teeth ('teef'), lots of shiny bitz, small pet hair Squig.

Traits: Auto-stabilised, Brutal Charge, Fear (1), Make It Work, Might Makes Right, Mob Rule, Size (Hulking), Sturdy, Unnatural Strength (x2), Unnatural Toughness (x2)

Source: Mark of the Xenos, p. 57

system.benefit: Remove bracing requirement for Heavy weapons
system.description: The character can wield Heavy weapons as if they were Basic weapons.
The character adds +2 to his damage rolls in melee.

system.benefit: Spend Reaction for extra melee attack after All Out Attack
system.description: If the character successfully hits with an All Out Attack action, the character may spend a Reaction to make an additional melee attack.

system.benefit: Recover damage as if Lightly Damaged
system.description: The character's constitution rebounds quickly from shock or injury. When undergoing medical treatment or healing from injuries, the character recovers Damage as if Lightly Damaged.
If the character is ever Stunned, the character may make a Toughness Test to overcome the effects. If the character succeeds, the Stunned condition is removed.

system.benefit: Make two melee attacks as Full Action
system.description: As a Full Action, the character may make two melee attacks.
The character has been schooled in the art of dirty fighting. When the character makes a melee attack and scores a hit, he may add +2 to the Critical Damage result.
Whenever the character suffers Critical Damage, halve the amount (rounding up).
The creature always counts as braced when firing heavy weapons and may fire on semi- or full-automatic as a Half Action.
A creature with this trait deals an extra 3 points of damage when it charges in the same round. A horde with this trait does an additional 1d10 damage when it charges in the same round.

A creature with this trait has an unnerving appearance. The fear of it always has an associated rating (1–4) to reflect just how awful the creature is to behold. See page 277 for the effects of Fear on Space Marines.

Unreliable Ork weapons are not Unreliable in an Ork’s hands. Ork technology defies the laws of physics and engineering, functioning through a combination of brute force and the latent psychic field generated by the Ork race. Ork weapons and equipment, while crude and unreliable in the hands of other races, work just fine for Orks. An Ork never suffers penalties for using Ork-made equipment.
Amongst Orks, Intimidate can be substituted for Command Tests.

All Orks are latently psychic, an ability that increases in strength the more of them there are in one place. This bolsters their confidence and courage to near-fearless levels when they gather en masse. For every additional Ork within 10m, the Ork’s Willpower gains a +10 bonus to resist the effects of Fear and Pinning. In a Horde, Orks are immune to Fear and Pinning.
This creature is Hulking. Enemies gain +10 to hit it, and it suffers –10 to Concealment. Its base movement is Agility Bonus +1.
Sturdy creatures are hard to move and thus gain a +20 bonus to tests made to resist grappling and uses of the Takedown Talent.

The creature's Strength Characteristic is unnatural. Each time this trait is gained, double its Strength Bonus. For example, a creature with Strength 41 normally has a 4 Strength Bonus. With this trait, its Strength Bonus increases to 8.

The trait may be gained multiple times. Each time you apply this trait to Strength, the Bonus multiplier increases by 1. For example, one selection multiplies the Strength Bonus by ×2, two selections by ×3, and three selections ×4. Note this trait does not modify the creature's movement.

During Opposed Characteristic Tests, on a success, the bonus multiplier is added to the degree of success. Any additional multipliers for Unnatural Characteristics (i.e., if a Space Marine with Unnatural Strength (x2) uses a Powerfist, which doubles the user's Strength Bonus), simply improves the existing multiplier by 1. For a Space Marine using a Powerfist, he would triple his Strength Bonus rather than doubling it and then doubling it again.

When taking a Skill Test based upon Strength with the Unnatural trait, the base Difficulty of the Test is staged downwards one level for degree of multiplier past normal (to a maximum of +30).

During Opposed Characteristic Tests, where success is achieved, the Unnatural multiplier is added to the degrees of success. In the case of a tie, the participant without the Unnatural Characteristic trait loses the Opposed Test.

The creature's Toughness Characteristic is unnatural. Each time this trait is gained, double its Toughness Bonus. For example, a creature with Toughness 41 normally has a 4 Toughness Bonus. With this trait, its Toughness Bonus increases to 8.

The trait may be gained multiple times. Each time you apply this trait to Toughness, the Bonus multiplier increases by 1. For example, one selection multiplies the Toughness Bonus by ×2, two selections by ×3, and three selections ×4. Note this trait does not modify the creature's movement.

During Opposed Characteristic Tests, on a success, the bonus multiplier is added to the degree of success. Any additional multipliers for Unnatural Characteristics simply improve the existing multiplier by 1.

When taking a Skill Test based upon Toughness with the Unnatural trait, the base Difficulty of the Test is staged downwards one level for degree of multiplier past normal (to a maximum of +30).

During Opposed Characteristic Tests, where success is achieved, the Unnatural multiplier is added to the degrees of success. In the case of a tie, the participant without the Unnatural Characteristic trait loses the Opposed Test.
Massive suits of armour plate cobbled together by Ork Meks, mega-armour turns an already formidable Nob into a walking tank.
A pair of crude Ork firearms bolted together, spraying a hail of inaccurate but devastating fire.
A massive hydraulic claw crackling with barely-contained energy, capable of tearing through even Space Marine armour.
